turns.thought: [OBSERVE] The swarm is completely stalled at iteration 838 with no improvement for 200 iterations. Global best fitness is 2.8e-13, identical across all particles (fitness mean and worst equal to best, fitness std ~1e-28). Diversity has collapsed to ~1.2e-22 (normalized ~1.97e-25). Velocity zero ratio is 1.0, velocity direction consistency is 0.0. All particles are essentially at the same point with zero velocity, so the swarm cannot explore further. | [DIAGNOSE] This is a classic premature convergence. The swarm has collapsed to a single near-optimal but sub-optimal point (2.8e-13) due to excessive exploitation and complete loss of diversity. Since the sphere function is unimodal and smooth, the plateau near the optimum is flat enough that zero velocities prevent any further sliding toward the exact zero. The root cause is that earlier PSO dynamics allowed convergence before reaching machine-precision zero, and now the swarm is frozen. | [STRATEGIZE] The best approach is to introduce a very small perturbation to break the stall while preserving the excellent current solution. A global reinitialization would discard the near-optimal position and is unnecessary for a unimodal problem. Instead, use Gaussian mutation with an extremely small scale (relative to the search span) to add noise to a fraction of particles. This will restore local diversity and non‑zero velocities, allowing the swarm to perform fine‑grained exploitation around the current best and potentially reach the exact minimum.
